# README Failure Patterns

Use this reference to spot the kinds of README defects that survive basic proofreading.

## Content Failures

- vague descriptions that never say what the project actually does
- setup instructions that assume hidden local context
- examples that do not work when copied
- missing explanation of prerequisites, secrets, or external services
- outdated statements that no longer match the implementation

## Structural Failures

- large walls of text with weak heading hierarchy
- no table of contents when the README is long enough to need one
- broken internal links or references to missing docs
- decorative diagrams, screenshots, or badges with no explanatory value
- sections kept only because generic templates include them

## Tone Failures

- language that is too technical for the intended audience
- marketing fluff instead of practical setup guidance
- support or maintenance status that is unclear or misleading
- limitations omitted to make the project sound simpler than it is

## Review Trigger

If two or more of these patterns appear in the same README, switch from light polish to structural rewrite.
